2606.22574.title: The Power of Light: Improving Synthetic-to-Real Domain Adaptation through Physically-Based Indirect Illumination
2606.22574.paper_summary: While synthetic data generation resolves the manual labeling bottleneck in computer vision, minimizing the syn-to-real domain gap requires optimizing rendering variables. This paper presents a systematic study analyzing the impact of lighting configurations and background complexity on object detection performance. We introduce SmartSDG, an automated, reproducible pipeline built on NVIDIA Isaac Sim using Physically-Based Shading (PBS), alongside ILLUM\_INTRUCK, a new multi-object industrial benchmark dataset. Through 18 controlled experiments utilizing a state-of-the-art YOLOv12 framework, we demonstrate that complex, indirect lighting configurations paired with domain-relevant background variability significantly increase visual cue richness. Our quantitative findings show that avoiding direct specular peaks preserves crucial surface textures, mitigates the domain gap, reduces false positives, and accelerates model convergence compared to using conventional direct-light synthetic data. Ultimately, we provide actionable virtual scene design guidelines to maximize object detection robustness in industrial automation.
2606.22574.paper_summary_zh: 雖然合成數據生成解決了計算機視覺中的手動標記瓶頸，但最小化合成到真實的領域差距需要優化渲染變量。本文提出了一項系統性研究，分析了照明配置和背景複雜度對物體檢測性能的影響。我們介紹了SmartSDG，一個基於NVIDIA Isaac Sim、使用物理基礎著色（PBS）的自動化、可重複的流程，以及ILLUM\_INTRUCK，一個新的多物體工業基準數據集。通過18個控制實驗，利用最先進的YOLOv12框架，我們證明了複雜的間接照明配置與與領域相關的背景變異相結合，顯著增加了視覺線索的豐富性。我們的定量研究結果顯示，避免直接的鏡面峰值可以保留關鍵的表面紋理，減少領域差距，降低假陽性，並加速模型收斂，相較於使用傳統的直接光合成數據。最終，我們提供了可行的虛擬場景設計指南，以最大化工業自動化中的物體檢測穩健性。
2606.22574.author: Hooman Tavakoli Ghinani et.al.
2606.22574.authors: Hooman Tavakoli Ghinani, Tatjana Legler, Martin Ruskowski
